article.body_preview:
DHAKA - Bangladesh Bank (BB) today announced 'cautiously accommodative' Monetary Policy Statement (MPS) for the second half of the current fiscal year 2022-23 (H2FY23).

"BB's monetary and credit programs for H2FY23 will pursue a cautiously accommodative policy stance to contain inflationary and exchange rate pressures, support desired economic growth, ensuring the necessary flow of funds to the economy's productive and employment generating activities," said BB Governor Abdur Rouf Talukder whil

llm_connections.evidence_quote: BB's monetary policy also seeks to promote import-substituting economic activities and dissuade imports of non-essential commodities to reduce the exchange rate pressure
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly says discouraging non-essential imports is intended to "reduce the exchange rate pressure," which matches the feature's country-level exchange-rate volatility scope.

verification.quote_best_match: BB's monetary policy also seeks to promote import-substituting economic activities and dissuade imports of non-essential commodities to reduce the exchange rate pressure,
verification.judge_reasoning: The article contains the quoted sentence stating BB will 'dissuade imports of non-essential commodities to reduce the exchange rate pressure,' which explicitly links that action as intended to reduce country-level exchange-rate pressure.
